We believe this is the first observed mutual event between satellites of Uranus. Fitting a simple geometric model to the lightcurve, we measure the mid-event time with a precision of 4 seconds. We assume previously measured values for the albedos of the two satellites [@kar01], and measure the impact parameter to be $500 \pm 80$ km. These measurements are more precise than estimates based on current ephemerides for these satellites. Therefore, observations of additional mutual events during the 2007–2008 Uranian equinox will provide improved estimates of their orbital and physical parameters. Keywords: Minor planets, planets and satellites: individual: Uranus, techniques: photometric 1. Introduction The Uranian satellite system offers a unique opportunity to study the dynamics and physical properties of a planetary system with a large obliquity. The mutual events between Uranian satellites, such as occultations, can provide valuable data on their orbits and albedos. The telescope was equipped with a SBIG STL-11000M CCD camera, and the observations were performed in the V-band. The lightcurve of the occultation was recorded with a time resolution of 0.5 seconds. 3. Dust devil tracks Many areas on Mars experience the passage of giant dust devils. A thin coating of fine bright dust covers most of the Martian surface. When a dust devil goes by it blows away the coating and exposes the underlying dark surface. Dust devils have been seen from the ground and high overhead from orbit. They have even blown the dust off of the solar panels of the two Rovers on Mars, thereby greatly extending their lives. The twin Rovers were designed to last for 3 months, instead they have lasted for years and are still going. Opportunity Rover has lasted over 10 years and is still sending back data as of August 2017. The pattern of the tracks have been shown to change every few months. This genetic material is encapsulated within an icosahedral capsid, which consists of three distinct proteins: VP1, VP2, and VP3 [@B2]. Among these, VP2 serves as the predominant structural component; it significantly influences viral host range selection and tissue tropism. CPU time taken by that evaluation in seconds or milliseconds (depending on implementation). 3.
